title: The Hawkן¿½s Nest
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We checked our pace, the red road sharply rounding;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We heard the troubled flow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of the dark olive depths of pines resounding
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A thousand feet below.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Above the tumult of the canyon lifted,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The gray hawk breathless hung,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or on the hill a winged shadow drifted
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Where furze and thorn-bush clung;

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or where half-way the mountain side was furrowed
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With many a seam and scar;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or some abandoned tunnel dimly burrowed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A mole-hill seen so far.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We looked in silence down across the distant
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Unfathomable reach:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A silence broken by the guideן¿½s consistent
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And realistic speech.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Walker of Murphyן¿½s blew a hole through Peters
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For telling him he lied;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then up and dusted out of South Hornitos
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Across the Long Divide.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½We ran him out of Strongן¿½s, and up through Eden,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And ן¿½cross the ford below,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And up this canyon (Petersן¿½ brother leadinן¿½),
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And me and Clark and Joe.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He fouן¿½t us game: somehow I disremember
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Jest how the thing kem round;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Some say ן¿½twas wadding, some a scattered ember
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From fires on the ground.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½But in one minute all the hill below him
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Was just one sheet of flame;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Guardinן¿½ the crest, Sam Clark and I called to him,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And, well, the dog was game!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He made no sign: the fires of hell were round him,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The pit of hell below.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We sat and waited, but we never found him;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And then we turned to go.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And then you see that rock thatן¿½s grown so bristly
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With chapparal and tan
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Suthin crepן¿½ out: it might hev been a grizzly
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ It might hev been a man;

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Suthin that howled, and gnashed its teeth, and shouted
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In smoke and dust and flame;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Suthin that sprang into the depths about it,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Grizzly or man, but game!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Thatן¿½s all! Well, yes, it does look rather risky,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And kinder makes one queer
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And dizzy looking down. A drop of whiskey
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ainן¿½t a bad thing right here!ן¿½
meaning:
"The Hawkן¿½s Nest" is a quintessential example of Bret Harte (Francis)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
